title: Media Darling: T-shirt design
summary: I was looking through Comptiq, Vol. 41, April 1988 and spotted an illustration showing the relative size of 2” floppy disks —used in some word processors at the time—compared to more common floppy disk sizes at the time: 8”, 5”, and 3.5”. Which got me thinking about the ever decreasing size of storage and how it might make for a nice illustration on a T-shi…
content:
I was looking through Comptiq, Vol. 41, April 1988 and spotted an illustration showing the relative size of 2” floppy disks—used in some word processors at the time—compared to more common floppy disk sizes at the time: 8”, 5”, and 3.5”.

Which got me thinking about the ever decreasing size of storage and how it might make for a nice illustration on a T-shirt. So I created technical drawings of the following media in decreasing size:

  • 5.25” floppy disk
  • 3.5” floppy disk
  • MiniDisc
  • CompactFlash
  • SecureDigital
  • MicroSD

T-shirt

Various sizes and colours.

  • gingerbeardman.creator-spring.com/listing/media-darling

Stickers (To Scale)

Roughly to scale, on clear vinyl.

  • gingerbeardman.creator-spring.com/listing/media-darling-decal-floppy

  • gingerbeardman.creator-spring.com/listing/media-darling-decal-minidisc

Sticker (Not To Scale)

Not to scale, on clear vinyl

  • gingerbeardman.creator-spring.com/listing/media-darling-decal-colour

Alt Store

Some buyers may choose to buy from my alternate web store which ships from the UK.

www.tshirtstudio.com/marketplace/gingerbeardman

This store has more limited colour choices, though you can edit that during checkout. YMMV.
